headline: CES 2026 showcases robotaxi and autonomous vehicle innovations
summary: Uber announced alliance with Lucid and Nuro leveraging Nvidia Alpamayo platform to deploy production-intent robotaxis

headline: Zoox receives federal approval to deploy robotaxis
summary: NHTSA exemption granted for purpose-built robotaxis without steering wheels or brake pedals. Production facility in California with goal of manufacturing up to 10,000 vehicles annually.

headline: Prof. Amnon Shashua at CES 2026: Robotaxi updates, breakthroughs in AI, and robotics
summary: MOIA aims to deploy over 100,000 self-driving vehicles by 2033 based on Mobileye's self-driving system. Initial U.S. deployments planned for 2026 followed by broader rollouts in Europe using ID. Buzz platform with Volkswagen and Mobileye Drive Level 4 autonomous driving

headline: Europe’s first industrially produced roboshuttle with Mobileye technology enters pre-series production
summary: Pre-series production of autonomous ID. Buzz with Mobileye technology started at VW Hanover plant. Around 500 vehicles planned for various projects in Europe and USA. Series production scheduled for 2027.
